comments.claim: `warden check` still validates provider environment before any deterministic checks run.
comments.explanation: The check command is described as deterministic-only and no-LLM, but the shared startup path calls `wardenEnv()` before acquiring the diff. Users without review credentials can be blocked before they reach the deterministic path.
comments.suggestedAction: Split required environment validation by mode so `check` only validates the variables it actually needs.

sources.title: check command description
sources.snippet: "Fast deterministic-only review (TSC + ESLint + npm audit + OSV verification). No LLM call. Suitable for pre-commit / CI gating.",

comments.claim: Should the quick-start distinguish the optional semantic index from the required review path?
comments.explanation: The README puts `pnpm warden init` before both `check` and `review`, while the environment table says `VOYAGE_API_KEY` is required for init and review falls back to cheap signals when it is unset. Readers may interpret embeddings as mandatory for every review.
comments.suggestedAction: Clarify that `init` improves review context quality but the CLI can still run without the semantic index.

sources.title: README Voyage fallback note
sources.snippet: | `VOYAGE_API_KEY`               | Required for `warden init`. Enables the semantic context selector in `warden review` (ADR-0019); when unset, review falls back to cheap signals only. |

comments.claim: An empty chunk store is treated as a missing index before model metadata is inspected.
comments.explanation: This is the right ordering for the banner: a repository with no chunks should produce a direct `no-index` action instead of falling through into model-age or embedding checks that cannot be meaningful yet.
comments.suggestedAction: Keep the `chunkCount === 0` guard ahead of locked-model reads when editing this path.

degradedWorkers.message: context: semantic index missing - run warden init to enable embedding-backed context selection.
